flags.justification: A broad, mandatory, quantified target applied government-wide across all covered agencies, not concentrated in one official.
Specific, quantified targets (4% minimum, 10% from senior levels) with specific milestone dates (25%/62.5%/100% across three fiscal years).
Sec. 4 defers the actual exemption criteria for essential services entirely to OMB's subsequent, undefined guidance.

A hard, quantified reduction target with no textual safeguard against gaming position classifications or reductions being achieved at the expense of service quality creates a plausible structural incentive concern common to quota-based downsizing mandates.
A one-time, three-year, definite-duration reduction program with explicit target dates through fiscal year 1995.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
No external check exists on OMB's exemption-granting discretion.
Part of a major, historically significant federal-workforce-reduction initiative associated with the 'reinventing government' National Performance Review effort.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
Sec. 4 delegates entirely open-ended exemption-granting authority to OMB with no defined criteria specified in this order's own text.
